   #include <hexi/hexi.h>
   #include <vector>
   #include <cstdint>
   #include <stdexcept> // for std::out_of_range

   std::vector<uint8_t> peek_buffer_data(const std::vector<uint8_t>& buffer, size_t bytes_to_peek) {
        if (bytes_to_peek > buffer.size()) {
            throw std::out_of_range("bytes_to_peek exceeds buffer size");
        }

        hexi::buffer_adaptor<std::vector<uint8_t>> adaptor(const_cast<std::vector<uint8_t>&>(buffer));
        std::vector<uint8_t> result(bytes_to_peek);
        adaptor.copy(result.data(), bytes_to_peek);
        return result;
   }

   #include <hexi/hexi.h>
   #include <vector>
   #include <cstdint>
   #include <stdexcept>

   std::vector<uint8_t> peek_buffer_data(const std::vector<uint8_t>& buffer, size_t bytes_to_peek) {
        if (bytes_to_peek > buffer.size()) {
            throw std::out_of_range("bytes_to_peek exceeds buffer size");
        }

        // Create a buffer_adaptor object from the buffer, removing constness
        hexi::buffer_adaptor<std::vector<uint8_t>> adaptor(const_cast<std::vector<uint8_t>&>(buffer));

        // Create a destination vector
        std::vector<uint8_t> result(bytes_to_peek);

        // Copy the data without advancing the read pointer
        adaptor.copy(result.data(), bytes_to_peek);

        return result;
   }

```cpp
#include <hexi/hexi.h>
#include <vector>
#include <cstdint>
#include <cstddef>

const uint8_t* get_read_pointer(const std::vector<uint8_t>& buffer) {
    // Create a buffer_adaptor object from the buffer
    hexi::buffer_adaptor<std::vector<uint8_t>, false> adaptor(const_cast<std::vector<uint8_t>&>(buffer));
    return adaptor.read_ptr();
}

uint8_t* get_write_pointer(std::vector<uint8_t>& buffer) {
    // Create a buffer_adaptor object from the buffer
    hexi::buffer_adaptor<std::vector<uint8_t>, false> adaptor(buffer);
    return adaptor.write_ptr();
}

struct BufferInfo {
    size_t readable_size;
    size_t free_space;
};

BufferInfo check_buffer_space(const std::vector<uint8_t>& buffer) {
    // Create a buffer_adaptor object from the buffer by removing constness
    std::vector<uint8_t>& non_const_buffer = const_cast<std::vector<uint8_t>&>(buffer);
    hexi::buffer_adaptor<std::vector<uint8_t>, false> adaptor(non_const_buffer);

    // Get the read and write pointers
    const uint8_t* read_ptr = adaptor.read_ptr();
    uint8_t* write_ptr = adaptor.write_ptr();

    // Calculate the indices
    const uint8_t* buffer_data = non_const_buffer.data();
    size_t read_index = read_ptr - buffer_data;
    size_t write_index = static_cast<const uint8_t*>(write_ptr) - buffer_data;

    // Calculate the readable size and free space
    BufferInfo info;
    info.readable_size = write_index - read_index;
    info.free_space = non_const_buffer.size() - write_index;

    return info;
}
```

<think>
We are given the structure PaddedData and need to serialize it into a vector<uint8_t>.
 Steps:
 1. Create a Hexi::Buffer object to hold the serialized data.
 2. Create a Hexi::BinaryStream object using the buffer.
 3. Use the stream's operator<< to write the header and data (which will automatically handle the length prefix for the string).
 4. Use the stream's fill<16>(0x00) method to write 16 bytes of padding.
 5. Use the stream's operator<< to write the footer.
